summary: Shard B is coherent as a contract/technical extraction shard if executed as two labeled lanes: 3 ordinary single-document extraction cases plus 13 selected-project contract/technical cases that must receive fixed project seeds before answer execution. No rows should be removed or swapped before the first frozen run.

models.review_method: Read-only local artifact inspection and proposal writing; no answer generation, retriever, evaluator, judge, or paid API/model call was run.

budget_summary.proposed_next_execution: lane-specific frozen answer run after seed and sidecar preflight
budget_summary.budget_note: This worker did not estimate paid execution cost because it did not run the answer harness.

outputs.description: Proposal-only review of shard B case placement, seed requirements, sidecar metrics, and scoring risks.

proposal.bottom_line: Accept shard B as currently composed. Keep Q024, Q026, Q027 as ordinary single-document extraction controls and keep Q038-Q050 as one selected-project contract/technical mini-suite. Do not merge these rows into one undifferentiated EDD claim, and do not execute Q038-Q050 until the seed project identity is fixed and logged.

accepted_recommendations.reason: The shard consistently tests procurement contract fields and technical requirement extraction. The first three rows are concrete single-document controls; the remaining rows are selected-project extraction questions spanning bid, contract, schedule, deliverable, maintenance, API, infrastructure, data, UI/UX, and security requirements.

lane_groups.execution_requirement: No seed turn required, but preflight body-visible support and exact target document identity before strict scoring.
A fixed selected-project seed is required before every answer. Record the seed project title, organization, source document identifiers, and seed-turn text in the runnable manifest.

accepted_recommendations.reason: A combined aggregate score would hide the different failure modes: ordinary rows mainly test target retrieval and missing claims, while selected-project rows mainly test context retention and field-level extraction.
The shard plan and gap review both state that these are diagnostic candidates until the runnable file, manifest, and exposure entry are fixed before first answer inspection.

watch_but_keep.risk: It overlaps conceptually with C shard Q053 and Q071 because all touch external integration or data migration.
watch_but_keep.reason: Q046 is a primary selected-project technical extraction row; C's related rows continue technical follow-up or boundary behavior, so moving Q046 would weaken B's contract-to-technical coverage.

watch_but_keep.risk: It overlaps with Q026 on accessibility terms.
watch_but_keep.reason: Q026 is a concrete single-document accessibility consulting control, while Q049 asks for selected-project UI/UX requirements including screens, responsiveness, accessibility, and convenience.

watch_but_keep.risk: Business amount fields may be metadata-only or coarse in some RFP bodies.
watch_but_keep.reason: Budget/amount extraction is core procurement behavior, but strict scoring must not punish the answer for CSV-only fields unless the field is visible in retrievable RFP text.

minimum_seed_record: seed_turn_text given to the system before the question
whether each expected field is in RFP body text, metadata only, or absent
seed_project_requirements_before_executi.recommended_seed_strategy: Use one fixed, body-rich selected RFP for the 13 selected-project cases if it visibly contains contract, schedule, deliverable, maintenance, technical, UI, data, and security sections. If no single project covers those fields, split into two declared seed cohorts: one contract/procurement seed for Q038-Q045 and one technical seed for Q046-Q050. Do not let the retriever silently choose a different project per question.

non_seed_case_preflight.requirement: Confirm 부산국제영화제 BIFF & ACFM 온라인서비스 재개발 body chunks include submission method or proposal-condition text.
Confirm 한국재정정보원 e나라도움 업무시스템 웹 접근성 컨설팅 body chunks include consulting scope text.
Confirm 대한적십자사 의료원 병원정보 재해복구시스템 구축 body chunks include DR or backup requirements.

sidecar_metrics_before_execution.pass_rule: The answer must stay on the seeded project; retrieved chunks and cited evidence should match the seed project identity.
No budget, schedule, requirement, deliverable, or technical field may be transferred from a different project.
For each case, define 2-5 required claim slots before execution and fail the sidecar if answer omits a retrievable requested field.
When the seeded RFP lacks a requested field, the answer should state that the field was not found in the provided/retrieved document instead of fabricating it.
If a retrievable exact deadline, score, amount, period, start date, completion date, or inspection schedule exists, the answer should include the exact value rather than a coarse paraphrase.

If amount or budget caution is only in CSV/list metadata and not RFP body text, mark the case metadata_diagnostic or provide separate metadata tooling; do not count it as a strict RFP-body answering miss.
Before the run, label each expected field as body_visible, metadata_visible, absent, or ambiguous.
Keep normal answer latency under the current gate target and flag any single case above 30 seconds even if average latency passes.

case_level_notes.review: Coherent ordinary control for submission/proposal-condition extraction. Risk is answer drifting into generic procurement checklist instead of BIFF & ACFM evidence.
Coherent ordinary control for domain-specific accessibility consulting scope. Risk is hallucinating generic WCAG/accessibility obligations beyond stated consulting scope.
Coherent ordinary control for DR/backup extraction. Risk is over-broad security summary if DR or backup phrases are sparse.
Keep as selected-project bid-submission extraction. Requires exact deadline/document-list availability preflight.
Keep as selected-project evaluation-criteria extraction. Must distinguish proposal evaluation criteria from final award result or actual scores.
Keep as selected-project contract-type extraction. Must not infer negotiated/emergency/rebid status from title alone unless source evidence supports it.
Keep as selected-project eligibility/restriction extraction. Needs required-claim slots for qualifications, restrictions, and caveats.
Keep with preflight. It is low difficulty only if exact amount/budget caveat is body-visible or metadata tooling is allowed.
Keep as schedule/period extraction. Must support partial absence: answer period if present and caveat missing start/completion/inspection dates.
Keep as deliverable/inspection extraction. Risk is missing non-obvious deliverables such as reports, designs, test results, manuals, training, or operation documents.
Keep as maintenance/operation support extraction. Must distinguish warranty/free maintenance period from ongoing operation support scope.
Keep as selected-project integration/API/data-transfer extraction. Overlaps with C follow-up rows but is the right primary technical gate for B.
Keep as infrastructure architecture extraction. Sidecar should catch invented cloud/server/security-equipment requirements.
Keep as backend/data requirement extraction. Define required claim slots carefully because the prompt bundles database, code, statistics, logs, and history management.
Keep as frontend/UI requirement extraction. Should not be moved to Q026's accessibility lane because this asks selected-project UI/UX breadth.
Keep as high-difficulty security checklist extraction. Must avoid generic security best-practice padding and cite only 개인정보, 권한관리, 로그관리, 암호화, 취약점 점검 evidence.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Run Q038-Q050 without a seed project
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: The target is undefined, so high EDD could reward whichever project the retriever happens to surface.
rejected_or_risky_items.recommendation: block execution until seed_project_required is satisfied.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: One merged score for all 16 shard B cases
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: The ordinary controls and selected-project rows have different answerability sources and sidecar blockers.
rejected_or_risky_items.recommendation: report at least ordinary_rag_text and selected_project subrows, then optionally show a labeled diagnostic aggregate.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Treat evaluation criteria as final evaluation or award result in Q039
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: This would look useful but invent unsupported procurement outcomes.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Use body-RAG only for Q042 if amount is metadata-only
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: The answer may be penalized for data the retriever cannot see, or may fabricate an amount.
rejected_or_risky_items.recommendation: preflight body visibility and mark metadata_diagnostic when appropriate.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Generic technical best-practice answers for Q046-Q050
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Judge relevance may appear high while the answer lacks source-specific claims.
rejected_or_risky_items.recommendation: make required claims and missing_required_claim sidecar override judge-only pass.

overfit_and_scoring_risks: The selected-project mini-suite reuses the same context pattern for 13 rows; if one seed is used, results measure deep extraction from that seed more than broad generalization.
If two seeds are used, the run becomes less compact but more robust; seed splits must be declared before answer execution to avoid post-hoc cherry-picking.
Case wording often asks 'if present' or '있는지', so whole-answer refusal is usually wrong when some fields are supported; field-level scoring is needed.
High groundedness/relevance judge scores can miss exact-value omissions, especially deadlines, amounts, periods, and score tables.
Current rows are diagnostic candidates, not strict validation, until frozen and registered before answer inspection.
merge_owner_actions: Add or require a runnable manifest field for selected_project_seed before executing Q038-Q050.
Preflight source availability for every exact or bundled field before strict scoring.
Report B as two lane groups and keep sidecar blockers beside EDD.
Do not update protected final question-bank files from this worker output directly; merge owner should decide.

commands_run: Python summary of all current input shard IDs, lanes, seed flags, segments, intents, and questions
observed_results: Shard B contains exactly 16 rows: Q024, Q026, Q027, and Q038-Q050.
Lane split is ordinary_rag_text=3 and conversation_or_selected_project_rag=13.
Q024, Q026, and Q027 have seed_required=false and answerability_source=rag_text.
Neighbor shard C starts the continuation/follow-up/boundary technical rows Q051-Q055 and Q067-Q073, so no B row needs to move there before first execution.
Neighbor shard D covers unsupported/persona/business/citation/instrumentation rows, so no B row needs to move there.
No paid API/model calls were made.
No answer harness, retriever, evaluator, or judge was run.
The target output file did not exist before this proposal was written.
